questionHtml:
Karl has three brown socks and four black socks in his drawer. He takes two socks at random from the drawer.

Find the probability that Karl takes two socks of the same colour.
markschemeHtml:
multiplying along branches and then adding outcomes         (M1)

37×26+47×36

=1842  =37≈0.429 42.9%             A1

 

[2 marks]

meta.title: Find the probability that Karl takes two socks of the same colour.
breadcrumbLabels: SL 4.6—Combined, mutually exclusive, conditional, independence, prob diagrams
